pageTitle.value: Martin Scorsese and Celina Murga - Video

pageCaption.value: For American master of cinema Martin Scorsese, to be a good film director, you need to become part of the world you are filming. His protégée, Argentinean director Celina Murga, does so with passion, he declares, describing her as a natural film-maker. The admiration is mutual. “Marty’s the perfect mentor,” says Murga.

description.value: Rolex Mentor and Protégée in Film, 2008 - 2009

text.value: For American master of cinema Martin Scorsese, to be a good film director, you need to become part of the world you are filming. His protégée, Argentinean director Celina Murga, does so with passion, he declares, describing her as a natural film-maker. The admiration is mutual. “Marty’s the perfect mentor,” says Murga.

pageTitle.value: Walter Murch and Sara Fgaier - Video

pageCaption.value: What does a film editor do? Walter Murch, editor of many iconic films, and his protégée, Italian film editor Sara Fgaier, provide succinct descriptions of the complex skill of constructing a feature film, the focus of their year-long mentorship.

WaPageTitle.value: Walter Murch and Sara Fgaier - Video

pageTitle.value: Zhang Yimou and Annemarie Jacir - Video

pageCaption.value: For Zhang Yimou, China’s creator of dazzling films, success is not about inspiration but the fruit “of hardships and efforts” as he meticulously masters every single scene. For his protégée Annemarie Jacir, working alongside him on set and in the editing room as he makes The Flowers of War, his supreme “visual sense” is the key to his rich and moving stories.

pageTitle.value: Jia Zhang-Ke and Rafael Manuel - Video

pageCaption.value: Emerging Filipino filmmaker Rafael Manuel collaborated for two years with acclaimed Chinese director Jia Zhang-Ke through the Rolex mentoring programme.

WaPageTitle.value: Jia Zhang-Ke and Rafael Manuel - Video

metadescription.value: Through the Rolex mentoring programme, emerging filmmaker Rafael Manuel collaborated for two years with director Jia Zhang-Ke. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

pageCaption.value: Declaring their mentorship to be “a process of development”, Wole Soyinka, first African Laureate of the Nobel Prize for Literature, encourages his protégée, Tara June Winch, as she agonizes over her writing. When anyone writes, he says, himself included, “it’s all over the place.” It’s the rewriting that follows that marks out a true writer.

WaPageTitle.value: Wole Soyinka and Tara June Winch - Video

metadescription.value: Declaring their mentorship to be “a process of development”, Wole Soyinka, first African Laureate of the Nobel Prize for Literature, encourages his protégée, Tara June Winch, as she agonizes over her writing. pageTitle.value: Youssou N’Dour and Aurelio Martínez - Video

pageCaption.value: A year’s mentorship with Youssou N’Dour gives Aurelio Martínez, from Honduras, not only an opportunity to boost his musical skills and self-confidence in the company of “the icon of African music”, but also to learn more about the historic and musical background of his African ancestors as he visits N’Dour’s continent for the first time.

pageTitle.value: Kate Valk and Nahuel Perez Biscayart - Video

pageCaption.value: Argentinian actor Nahuel Perez Biscayart shifts to New York to spend a year with his mentor, Kate Valk, a founding member of the Wooster Group. There he finds that inspiration comes as a result of producing, trying, failing and finding the beauty in accidents.
